title: Unsafe Functions and Traits
content:
An unsafe function has preconditions the compiler cannot verify. Callers must use an unsafe block, acknowledging they have ensured the preconditions. This is a documentation contract.

Unsafe traits have invariants that implementors must uphold. Send and Sync are the most common unsafe traits: incorrectly implementing them can cause data races.

The convention: document all safety requirements in a # Safety section in the doc comment. Explain exactly what the caller (or implementor) must guarantee.
keyPoints: unsafe fn means the function has preconditions the compiler cannot check
Document all safety requirements with a # Safety doc comment section
Safe wrappers ensure unsafe code's invariants are maintained
unsafe trait means implementors must uphold specific invariants
Send and Sync are unsafe traits -- incorrect impls cause data races
Prefer using safe abstractions from std (Mutex, atomic types) over writing your own
